summary: Validated TRUE POSITION ROLLOVER end-to-end: square-off arms _rollover_armed -> _autoload_after_roll triggers _rollover_enter which places a fresh SHORT (reason=EXPIRY_ROLLOVER) on the new contract. Verified setting toggle (true/false), market-closed guard, flat-at-expiry guard, reset() clears the flag, settings persist via API, and the UI checkbox renders/toggles. All prior expiry/market/exit-retry/session-health regression tests still green.
critical_code_review_comments: Note (non-blocking): _autoload_after_roll() clears _rollover_armed BEFORE awaiting _rollover_enter(); on a market-closed skip this means a future tick won't re-arm. Acceptable per spec ('rollover skipped — market closed'), but consider documenting that rollover does NOT carry across to the next session.
server.py is ~1280 lines — past the 700-line guideline; consider splitting TradingEngine into engine.py, expiry.py, risk.py, persistence.py for maintainability.
FastAPI @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') are deprecated — migrate to lifespan handlers (warnings emitted during tests).
seed_data_creation: In-process: monkey-patched engine.broker.connected=True, broker.fut_*, _expiry_status, _market_open, _execute_order. No DB seed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Rollover behaviour proven at unit-level only (broker order methods mocked). For a true live integration on next expiry day, watch _autoload_after_roll() flow and verify _rollover_enter alert appears, then ENTRY order with reason=EXPIRY_ROLLOVER hits Angel One. UI test toggled the checkbox during the run and restored it to true via /api/settings.
